industry: Data Infrastructure Semiconductors / Custom AI Silicon
thesis: Marvell is a mission-critical supplier to hyperscaler AI buildouts, with 76% of revenue from data center products including custom AI silicon (ASICs) and networking solutions. 27% revenue growth and 35% non-GAAP operating margins reflect strong execution, but the stock has run ~5x from 52-week lows and trades at ~97x P/E, creating significant valuation risk. Speculative Buy on pullback.

drivers: Custom AI silicon wins at 4+ hyperscalers on 3nm/2nm nodes
800G/1.6T AI networking upgrade cycle drives switch silicon share gains
DPU/SmartNIC adoption expands TAM beyond raw AI compute
Data center mix rises above 80%, pushing gross margins toward 60%+

risks: Hyperscaler AI capex cut triggers 30–40% multiple compression
Customer in-sourcing (Google TPU, Amazon Trainium) erodes custom silicon TAM
Broadcom bundles custom silicon with switching, squeezing Marvell share
NVIDIA GPU dominance limits ASIC adoption for training workloads

base_case.assumptions: Steady hyperscaler AI spend; custom silicon ramps at 2–3 customers; data center mix ~80%; gross margins stable at 58–62%; operating margins 35–38%.

catalysts.event: Q2 FY2027 earnings — EPS est $0.87; FY2027 guidance critical
Potential custom silicon design win announcements
Analyst day / investor event — margin and TAM updates

key_risks: Valuation: ~97x P/E and ~28x sales price in years of flawless execution
Customer concentration: AWS, Google, Microsoft represent large data center revenue share
Competition from Broadcom (AVGO) with deeper resources and larger installed base
